seoTitle: How to Get a Jira API Token: Setup Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to generate a Jira API token from your Atlassian account settings, save it securely, and revoke it when it's no longer needed.
intro: Connecting scripts, AI agents, or third-party tools to Jira requires an API token instead of your account password. This guide covers finding the token settings in your Atlassian profile, generating a new token, and revoking it later if it's no longer needed.

steps.heading: Open your account settings
steps.body: Click your profile avatar in the top right corner of Jira and select Account Settings to reach your Atlassian profile configuration.

steps.heading: Navigate to the API token section
steps.body: Go to the Security tab and scroll down to find the API token section, then click the link to manage your tokens.

steps.heading: Create a new token
steps.body: Click Create API Token, type a clear label describing what the token is for, and click Create to generate it.

steps.heading: Copy and store the token securely
steps.body: Copy the raw token string immediately and save it in a secure password manager, since it won't be shown again after you close the window.

steps.heading: Revoke tokens you no longer use
steps.body: Return to your active tokens list anytime and click Revoke next to an entry to remove access for tokens that are outdated or no longer needed.

faq.question: Where do I generate a Jira API token?
faq.answer: Go to your Atlassian account settings, open the Security tab, and use the Create and Manage API Tokens link to generate one.

faq.question: Can I see my Jira API token again after I close the window?
faq.answer: No, you need to copy the token immediately when it's generated and store it securely, since it won't be displayed again afterward.

faq.question: How do I use a Jira API token in a script?
faq.answer: You typically pass the token inside an authorization header alongside your account email when authenticating API requests.

faq.question: How do I revoke a Jira API token?
faq.answer: Go to your active tokens dashboard and click Revoke next to the token you want to remove access for.
